name: Charleston Postal
description_short: Charleston Postal Federal Credit Union is West Virginia's oldest credit union (founded 1925), serving postal employees, retirees, and their families across the KY/WV postal district with competitive loan and financial services.
description_long:
Charleston Postal Federal Credit Union was established in 1925 and holds the distinction of being the oldest credit union in West Virginia. It operates as a member-owned financial cooperative with a specific membership mandate: current and retired postal employees within the KY/West Virginia district postal area and their immediate families, plus credit union employees. The institution is headquartered in Charleston, WV, and maintains traditional banking hours Monday to Friday, 7 a.m. to 3 p.m.

The credit union offers a range of lending products including auto loans (both new and used vehicles), recreational vehicle loans, and personal loans. Current advertised rates include new auto loans as low as 5.22% APR, used auto loans at 6.45% APR, RV loans at 9.25% APR, and personal loans at 12.00% APR. Members can apply for loans by submitting their last two months of paystubs in PDF format via email. The institution also provides standard banking services including debit cards, visa credit cards, and member accounts.

What distinguishes Charleston Postal FCU is its 100+ year history, member-ownership structure, and exclusive focus on the postal employee community and their families. Unlike traditional banks, it operates on a non-profit cooperative model where members are owners. The credit union emphasizes low-cost, competitive financial services tailored to postal workers' needs and maintains personal relationships through its small team and direct contact information for officers.

The main caveat is that membership is restricted to postal employees and their immediate families—this is not a credit union open to the general public. Additionally, the credit union operates limited hours (7 a.m.–3 p.m. weekdays only) and has only one physical location, which may create accessibility challenges for members outside the immediate Charleston, WV area. The organization appears to be modest in size with a small staff.
services: New auto loans (as low as 5.22% APR)
Used auto loans (as low as 6.45% APR)
Recreational vehicle loans (as low as 9.25% APR)
Debit cards with lost/stolen card reporting (888-263-3370)
Visa credit cards with dedicated lost/stolen reporting (800-325-3678 or 800-247-4222)
Online loan applications and document submission
Member annual meetings with voting rights for officer elections
Financial services consultation via phone and email
pros: West Virginia's oldest credit union (founded 1925) with 100+ year track record and stability
Member-owned cooperative structure means members are owners, not customers of a profit-driven entity
Competitive auto loan rates: as low as 5.22% for new autos, 6.45% for used vehicles
Exclusive membership for postal employees and families provides community focus and tailored services
Low-cost financial services mission explicitly stated as primary purpose
Personal service with direct contact to managers and staff by phone and email
No pictures/no special formatting required for loan applications—simple PDF submission of paystubs
cons: Membership is restricted to postal employees, retirees, and immediate family only—not open to general public
Very limited hours of operation (7 a.m.–3 p.m. weekdays only) may be inconvenient for many members
Only one physical location in Charleston, WV with no branch offices—limiting accessibility
Personal loan rate of 12.00% is notably higher than auto lending rates and above many competitors
Website lacks details on savings accounts, CDs, or other deposit products beyond loan offerings
best_for: Current and retired USPS employees in the KY/West Virginia postal district seeking member-owned credit union lending
Postal workers' immediate family members needing auto loans or personal financing with competitive rates
Members prioritizing long-term stability and non-profit cooperative structure over large bank convenience
Charleston, WV-area postal employees who value personal relationships with credit union staff
diagnosis: Charleston Postal FCU is best for USPS employees and their immediate families in Kentucky and West Virginia seeking competitive auto and personal loans through a stable, member-owned credit union. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—you must be a postal employee or their family member to join; this institution is not accessible to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
